title: tradescantia scaberrima
intro: Tradescantia scaberrima, also known as the rough-leaf spiderwort, is a type of flowering plant native to Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which are derived from its leaves and roots. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues. Tradescantia scaberrima can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments.
